title: chamaesyce hirtula
intro: Chamaesyce hirtula, also known as hairy spurge or hairy sandmat, is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Chamaesyce hirtula can be prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, with the leaves and stems being used in traditional herbal remedies.
